title: Rootless systemd quadlets: Error: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/devices/user.slice/runtime: permission denied
body:
### Issue Description

When starting any podman quadlet, no matter what params, the service will fail with the error: 

`Error: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/devices/user.slice/runtime: permission denied`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create a systemd-container unit on `~/.config/containers/systemd/my-container.container`

```
[Unit]
Description=My container

[Container]
Image=localhost/my-container:latest
EnvironmentFile=/var/www/my-container/.env
#GlobalArgs=--cgroup-manager=systemd
#PublishPort=127.0.0.1:9090:8080

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

2. Reload systemd daemons: `systemctl --user daemon-reload`
3. Start service with: `systemctl --user start my-container.service`

### Describe the results you received

Apparently permissions problems with cgroups. SELinux is disabled.

```
● my-container.service - my container python backend
   Loaded: loaded (/home/myuser/.config/containers/systemd/my-container.container; generated)
   Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Tue 2024-07-02 18:18:22 UTC; 37min ago
  Process: 217732 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=/run/user/1002/my-container.cid (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
  Process: 217702 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name=systemd-my-container --cidfile=/run/user/1002/my-container.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d --publish 127.0.0.1:9090:8080 --env-file /var/www/my-container/.env localhost/my-container:latest (code=exited, status=126)
 Main PID: 217702 (code=exited, status=126)

Jul 02 18:18:22 server1.mydomain.com my-container[217702]: Error: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/devices/user.slice/runtime: permission denied
Jul 02 18:18:22 server1.mydomain.com systemd[115012]: my-container.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=126/n/a
Jul 02 18:18:22 server1.mydomain.com systemd[115012]: my-container.service: Killing process 217714 (slirp4netns) with signal SIGKILL.
Jul 02 18:18:22 server1.mydomain.com systemd[115012]: my-container.service: Killing process 217716 (rootlessport) with signal SIGKILL.
Jul 02 18:18:22 server1.mydomain.com systemd[115012]: my-container.service: Killing process 217722 (exe) with signal SIGKILL.
Jul 02 18:18:22 server1.mydomain.com systemd[115012]: my-container.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Jul 02 18:18:22 server1.mydomain.com systemd[115012]: Failed to start my container python backend.
```

### Describe the results you expected

Service should have started with `systemctl --user start my-container.service`

```
● my-container.service - my container python backend
   Loaded: loaded (/home/myuser/.config/containers/systemd/my-container.container; generated)
   Active: active (running) since Tue 2024-07-02 18:18:22 UTC; 37min ago
  Process: 217732 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=/run/user/1002/my-container.cid (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
  Process: 217702 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name=systemd-my-container --cidfile=/run/user/1002/my-container.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d --publish 127.0.0.1:9090:8080 --env-file /var/www/my-container/.env localhost/my-container:latest (code=exited, status=126)
 Main PID: 217702 (conmon)

Jul 02 18:18:22 server1.mydomain.com systemd[115012]: Started my container python backend.
```



### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

I tried with setting in `~/.config/containers/containers.conf`:

```
[engine]
cgroup_manager = "systemd"
```

But for some reason, `podman` won't honor the config.

I also added to the container's unit file `GlobalArgs=--cgroup-manager=systemd` but it didn't work neither.


### Additional information

I tested on Fedora 39 and it worked perfectly:

```
podman version: 4.9.4, 
systemd version: 254
kernel    version: 6.9.7
```

AlmaLinux 8.10 systemD version:

`systemd 239 (239-82.el8) +PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX +IMA -APPARMOR +SMACK +SYSVINIT +UTMP +LIBCRYPTSETUP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +ACL +XZ +LZ4 +SECCOMP +BLKID +ELFUTILS +KMOD +IDN2 -IDN +PCRE2 default-hierarchy=legacy`

comments.body: quadlet does not support cgroupv1 systems you have to use cgroupv2
